markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: **GTA VI’s confirmed November 19, 2026 console launch—after delays from Fall 2025 to May 2026 then November—sets a firm five-month window for this multi-outcome market.** Traders focus on verifiable events like album drops, geopolitical shifts, or celebrity milestones that could resolve specific options before the date. Recent Take-Two earnings commentary and insider reports signal no further slips, boosting confidence in the timeline while summer marketing ramps up. High-volume outcomes tied to music releases (e.g., Playboi Carti or Drake projects) and ongoing conflicts reflect real-time news flow and chart trajectories, with market-implied odds shifting on streaming data, official announcements, or confirmed reports. Precursor signals such as label statements or earnings calls remain key swing factors in the final months.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, including the May 2026 deadlock on the Jessica Mann count, combined with ongoing appeals of his 2022 California conviction, have driven trader consensus toward “No Prison Time” as the dominant outcome. Weinstein’s documented health decline, including cancer and heart issues, further supports the possibility of compassionate release or vacated sentences, outweighing the consecutive 16-year Los Angeles term that currently keeps him incarcerated. Historical precedent for overturned #MeToo-era convictions and prosecutorial decisions on whether to pursue a fourth New York trial after the June 24 hearing add uncertainty that favors the market-implied frontrunner. Upcoming appellate rulings and any renewed charges could shift momentum, though the current legal trajectory favors release over additional extended sentencing.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France leads the 2026 World Cup winner market after Spain’s 0-0 group-stage draw with Cape Verde tightened the race among elite European sides. Les Bleus benefit from exceptional squad depth featuring Kylian Mbappé and strong recent form, while Spain’s Euro 2024 momentum and Lamine Yamal’s emergence keep La Roja close behind. England, Portugal, Argentina as defending champions, and Germany each possess balanced attacking options, experienced coaches, and favorable early fixtures that sustain competitive implied probabilities near the top. The expanded 48-team format and ongoing group matches create volatility, with minor results like Ivory Coast’s upset win already shifting underdog odds, yet the concentration of talent among the leading nations prevents any single contender from pulling away decisively.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and the military as of mid-2026, with recent purges of senior PLA officers and Central Committee members widely viewed as reinforcing rather than undermining his authority. Official activities, including his 2026 New Year address, meetings with foreign leaders, and direction of the Central Economic Work Conference, underscore continuity in his leadership role ahead of the 21st Party Congress. Analysts note the absence of any designated successor or visible internal factions capable of forcing an early exit, consistent with his prior consolidation of power that removed term limits and elevated loyalists. These factors underpin trader consensus that removal or resignation before 2027 remains unlikely, absent unforeseen health or political shocks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading trader consensus in the 2028 Democratic nomination market due to his high national profile as California governor, visible Trump criticism, recent book tour, and early positioning moves such as hiring a consumer regulator focused on AI. This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading trader consensus in the 2028 Democratic nomination market due to his high national profile as California governor, visible Trump criticism, recent book tour, and early positioning moves such as hiring a consumer regulator focused on AI. A fragmented field leaves Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez with progressive support among younger voters, while Jon Ossoff, Kamala Harris, Josh Shapiro, and Pete Buttigieg trail amid competing electability arguments. The wide-open race after 2024 losses means consolidation hinges on 2026 midterm results, primary-state travel, polling shifts among key voting blocs, and any formal announcements, with no candidate yet locking in broad institutional backing.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading trader consensus in the 2028 Democratic nomination market due to his high national profile as California governor, visible Trump criticism, recent book tour, and early positioning moves such as hiring a consumer regulator focused on AI. A fragmented field leaves Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez with progressive support among younger voters, while Jon Ossoff, Kamala Harris, Josh Shapiro, and Pete Buttigieg trail amid competing electability arguments. The wide-open race after 2024 losses means consolidation hinges on 2026 midterm results, primary-state travel, polling shifts among key voting blocs, and any formal announcements, with no candidate yet locking in broad institutional backing.
